questions.specification: To ensure that all facets of a new software interface are considered, a company assembles a project group consisting of a systems architect, a cognitive psychologist, and a graphic artist. Which principle of the UCD process is primarily being applied?

To gain insights into how a new coffee machine should be designed, researchers spend a day in a busy cafe, silently recording the movements and interactions of the baristas. 

Which user-centred research strategy is being utilized?
Which of the following strategies is most effective for gathering user feedback on an early design concept during the early stages of user-centred design (UCD)?

options.markscheme: Surveys can support early user research, but they rely on self-reported opinions and are less effective for feedback on a specific design concept than observing users interacting with a prototype.

options.content: Analyzing market trends and sales data
options.markscheme: This is market research and does not directly gather user feedback on a design concept in a UCD process.

options.content: Performing user testing with low-fidelity prototypes
options.markscheme: Early low-fidelity prototype testing lets designers observe real user interaction, identify usability issues and pain points, and obtain actionable feedback to iterate the design—core to UCD.

options.content: Holding brainstorming sessions with the design team
options.markscheme: Brainstorming is an internal ideation method and does not collect feedback from users.

questions.specification: Which of the following is a primary method used in User-Centred Design (UCD) to gather user feedback?
Which of the following is a primary consideration in User-Centered Design (UCD)?

options.content: Ensuring the product is easy to use and meets the user's needs.
Maximizing the aesthetic appeal of the product.

questions.specification:
SoundNest is a company developing a new mobile app called **CalmCampus** to help university students manage stress.

![Screenshot mock-up of the CalmCampus home screen showing: a large "Start 2‑minute reset" button, a small "Track mood" link, a bottom navigation bar with icons for Home, Tools, Journal, and Settings, and a banner reading "Streak: 5 days".](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/a26c4c2f-32c4-414b-b238-f0fa70554c9a.png)

Before building the final app, the team ran a short **user-centred design (UCD)** study and summarised findings in Figure 2.

**Figure 2: Summary of early UCD findings**

![Infographic titled "CalmCampus: early user research snapshot" with icons and statistics: "24 students interviewed"; "6 students observed using a competitor app"; "Top pain points: (1) too many steps to start a session, (2) unclear labels, (3) notifications feel pushy"; "Accessibility notes: 5 users rely on screen readers; 7 users prefer larger text"; "Context: 60% use the app one-handed while walking"; "Goal: start a calming exercise in under 10 seconds"; "Prototype testing: 2 rounds planned".](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/804a2f60-23d3-443a-9333-df2f59617119.png)

parts.content: List two activities that typically occur in a user-centred design (UCD) process.
parts.markscheme:
- Identify user needs through user research (e.g., interviews, observation, surveys) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Evaluate ideas with users (e.g., usability testing of prototypes) <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Max 2 marks</note>

parts.content: Outline how involving students early in the design cycle could influence the design decisions for CalmCampus.
parts.markscheme:
- Early user input helps define/validate requirements and success criteria based on real user goals and contexts <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Early feedback reduces rework by revealing issues before full development (e.g., navigation/wording/accessibility) <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Max 2 marks</note>

parts.content: Outline one UCD principle that SoundNest should apply when designing the CalmCampus home screen.
parts.markscheme:
- Design is based on an explicit understanding of users, tasks and environments (e.g., one-handed use while walking) and how this impacts layout/controls <marks>2 marks</marks>
_**OR**_
- Users are involved throughout design and development (e.g., iterative testing with students) and their feedback is used to make changes <marks>2 marks</marks>
_**OR**_
- The design is driven and refined by user-centred evaluation (use test results to revise the screen) with iterations based on findings <marks>2 marks</marks>
_**OR**_
- Address the whole user experience, including accessibility and emotional comfort (avoid “pushy” notifications) and ensure the interface supports these needs <marks>2 marks</marks>

<note>Max 2 marks</note>

parts.content: Explain how design guidance (e.g., style guides, accessibility guidelines, and platform conventions) could improve the usability of CalmCampus.
parts.markscheme:
- Using platform conventions/style guides increases familiarity, so users can predict where controls are and how they behave <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Accessibility guidance (e.g., text size, contrast, screen-reader labels) improves inclusivity and reduces barriers for users with differing needs <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Consistent components and terminology reduce cognitive load and errors, supporting faster task completion (e.g., start a session within 10 seconds) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Guidance provides standards the team can test against, enabling clearer evaluation and iteration during prototyping <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Max 4 marks</note>

questions.specification:
A design team developing a new infusion pump for hospitals creates a series of low-fidelity prototypes. They observe nurses using these prototypes in a simulated environment, identify specific navigation errors, and then modify the interface layout before conducting a second round of testing. 

Which core principle of User-Centred Design (UCD) is being applied through this sequence of activities?
Which stakeholder holds the primary responsibility for ensuring that a new invention satisfies market needs and user requirements throughout the development process?

**AquaPure**

A new company, AquaPure, aims to introduce a line of advanced water filtration systems for home use. These systems are designed to be eco-friendly, easy to install, and cost-effective. AquaPure wants to ensure that their product meets consumer needs and stands out in a competitive market. 

They are focusing on sustainable materials, innovative filtration technology, and user-friendly design. AquaPure needs to conduct thorough market research to ensure the product meets consumer needs and preferences.

parts.content: Identify **two** market research strategies that AquaPure could use to gather information about consumer preferences for water filtration systems.
parts.markscheme:
- Use surveys/questionnaires to collect data on desired features, material preferences and acceptable price range <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Use focus groups to gather qualitative feedback through discussion of preferences and opinions about water filtration systems <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how AquaPure could use perceptual mapping to position their water filtration system in the market.
parts.markscheme:
- Define perceptual mapping as a visual representation of consumer perceptions of products/brands in a market <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Explain that AquaPure plots competitor products against key attributes (e.g., price, sustainability, filtration performance) to see relative positioning and gaps <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Link the identified gap/desired position to how AquaPure can emphasise differentiating features in its marketing/design to appeal to the target market <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Describe **two** advantages of conducting a competitor analysis for AquaPure before launching their water filtration system.
parts.markscheme:
- Identify market opportunities by comparing competitors’ strengths/weaknesses to find ways to differentiate (features, quality, sustainability, price) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Reduce risk/improve decision-making by informing pricing, marketing and product features based on competitor strategies and positioning <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: AquaPure decides to conduct a usability testing session for their new water filtration system design. Outline the key steps they should follow in this process.
parts.markscheme:
- Define the objectives/criteria for the test (e.g., ease of installation, ease of use, satisfaction) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Recruit suitable participants from the target market and prepare realistic tasks/scenarios <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Run the session: brief participants, observe them completing tasks, and record issues/feedback <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Analyse findings, prioritise problems, and implement improvements (iterate if needed) <marks>1 mark</marks>
